root_text.an3.65:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu cārikaṁ caramāno mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ yena kesamuttaṁ nāma kālāmānaṁ nigamo tadavasari.
root_text.an3.65:1.4: “samaṇo khalu, bho, gotamo sakyaputto sakyakulā pabbajito kesamuttaṁ anuppatto.
root_text.an3.65:1.5: Taṁ kho pana bhavantaṁ gotamaṁ evaṁ kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggato:
root_text.an3.65:1.7: sādhu kho pana tathārūpānaṁ arahataṁ dassanaṁ hotī’”ti.
root_text.an3.65:2.1: Atha kho kesamuttiyā kālāmā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā appekacce bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu, appekacce bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu, sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu, appekacce yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu, appekacce nāmagottaṁ sāvetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu, appekacce tuṇhībhūtā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te kesamuttiyā kālāmā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an3.65:3.2: Te sakaṁyeva vādaṁ dīpenti jotenti, parappavādaṁ pana khuṁsenti vambhenti paribhavanti omakkhiṁ karonti.
root_text.an3.65:3.3: Aparepi, bhante, eke samaṇabrāhmaṇā kesamuttaṁ āgacchanti.
root_text.an3.65:3.4: Tepi sakaṁyeva vādaṁ dīpenti jotenti, parappavādaṁ pana khuṁsenti vambhenti paribhavanti omakkhiṁ karonti.
root_text.an3.65:3.5: Tesaṁ no, bhante, amhākaṁ hoteva kaṅkhā hoti vicikicchā:
root_text.an3.65:3.6: ‘ko su nāma imesaṁ bhavataṁ samaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ saccaṁ āha, ko musā’”ti?
root_text.an3.65:3.8: Kaṅkhanīyeva pana vo ṭhāne vicikicchā uppannā.
root_text.an3.65:4.1: Etha tumhe, kālāmā, mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā samaṇo no garūti.
root_text.an3.65:4.3: ‘ime dhammā akusalā, ime dhammā sāvajjā, ime dhammā viññugarahitā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvattantī’ti, atha tumhe, kālāmā, pajaheyyātha.
root_text.an3.65:5.2: lobho purisassa ajjhattaṁ uppajjamāno uppajjati hitāya vā ahitāya vā”ti?
root_text.an3.65:7.1: “Luddho panāyaṁ, kālāmā, purisapuggalo lobhena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto pāṇampi hanati, adinnampi ādiyati, paradārampi gacchati, musāpi bhaṇati, parampi tathattāya samādapeti, yaṁ sa hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.65:9.2: doso purisassa ajjhattaṁ uppajjamāno uppajjati hitāya vā ahitāya vā”ti?
root_text.an3.65:11.1: “Duṭṭho panāyaṁ, kālāmā, purisapuggalo dosena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto pāṇampi hanati, adinnampi ādiyati, paradārampi gacchati, musāpi bhaṇati, parampi tathattāya samādapeti, yaṁ sa hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.65:13.2: moho purisassa ajjhattaṁ uppajjamāno uppajjati hitāya vā ahitāya vā”ti?
root_text.an3.65:15.1: “Mūḷho panāyaṁ, kālāmā, purisapuggalo mohena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto pāṇampi hanati, adinnampi ādiyati, paradārampi gacchati, musāpi bhaṇati, parampi tathattāya samādapeti, yaṁ sa hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.65:17.1: “Taṁ kiṁ maññatha, kālāmā, ime dhammā kusalā vā akusalā vā”ti?
root_text.an3.65:23.1: “Samattā samādinnā ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvattanti, no vā?
root_text.an3.65:25.3: Mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā samaṇo no garūti.
root_text.an3.65:25.5: “ime dhammā akusalā, ime dhammā sāvajjā, ime dhammā viññugarahitā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvattantīti, atha tumhe, kālāmā, pajaheyyāthā”’ti,
root_text.an3.65:25.6: iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ, idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an3.65:26.1: Etha tumhe, kālāmā, mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā samaṇo no garūti.
root_text.an3.65:26.3: ‘ime dhammā kusalā, ime dhammā anavajjā, ime dhammā viññuppasatthā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā hitāya sukhāya saṁvattantī’ti, atha tumhe, kālāmā, upasampajja vihareyyātha.
root_text.an3.65:27.2: alobho purisassa ajjhattaṁ uppajjamāno uppajjati hitāya vā ahitāya vā”ti?
root_text.an3.65:29.1: “Aluddho panāyaṁ, kālāmā, purisapuggalo lobhena anabhibhūto apariyādinnacitto neva pāṇaṁ hanati, na adinnaṁ ādiyati, na paradāraṁ gacchati, na musā bhaṇati, na parampi tathattāya samādapeti, yaṁ sa hoti dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.65:33.1: “Taṁ kiṁ maññatha, kālāmā, ime dhammā kusalā vā akusalā vā”ti?
root_text.an3.65:39.1: “Samattā samādinnā hitāya sukhāya saṁvattanti no vā?
root_text.an3.65:41.3: Mā anussavena, mā paramparāya, mā itikirāya, mā piṭakasampadānena, mā takkahetu, mā nayahetu, mā ākāraparivitakkena, mā diṭṭhinijjhānakkhantiyā, mā bhabbarūpatāya, mā samaṇo no garūti.
root_text.an3.65:41.5: ime dhammā kusalā, ime dhammā anavajjā, ime dhammā viññuppasatthā, ime dhammā samattā samādinnā hitāya sukhāya saṁvattantīti, atha tumhe, kālāmā, upasampajja vihareyyāthā’ti,
root_text.an3.65:41.6: iti yaṁ taṁ vuttaṁ idametaṁ paṭicca vuttaṁ.
root_text.an3.65:42.1: Sa kho so, kālāmā, ariyasāvako evaṁ vigatābhijjho vigatabyāpādo asammūḷho sampajāno patissato mettāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati, tathā dutiyaṁ, tathā tatiyaṁ, tathā catutthaṁ, iti uddhamadho tiriyaṁ sabbadhi sabbattatāya sabbāvantaṁ lokaṁ mettāsahagatena cetasā vipulena mahaggatena appamāṇena averena abyāpajjhena pharitvā viharati.
root_text.an3.65:42.4: upekkhāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati, tathā dutiyaṁ, tathā tatiyaṁ, tathā catutthaṁ, iti uddhamadho tiriyaṁ sabbadhi sabbattatāya sabbāvantaṁ lokaṁ upekkhāsahagatena cetasā vipulena mahaggatena appamāṇena averena abyāpajjhena pharitvā viharati.
root_text.an3.65:43.1: Sa kho so, kālāmā, ariyasāvako evaṁ averacitto evaṁ abyāpajjhacitto evaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhacitto evaṁ visuddhacitto.
root_text.an3.65:43.2: Tassa diṭṭheva dhamme cattāro assāsā adhigatā honti.
root_text.an3.65:43.3: ‘Sace kho pana atthi paro loko, atthi sukatadukkaṭānaṁ kammānaṁ phalaṁ vipāko, athāhaṁ kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjissāmī’ti, ayamassa paṭhamo assāso adhigato hoti.
root_text.an3.65:44.1: ‘Sace kho pana natthi paro loko, natthi sukatadukkaṭānaṁ kammānaṁ phalaṁ vipāko, athāhaṁ diṭṭheva dhamme averaṁ abyāpajjhaṁ anīghaṁ sukhiṁ attānaṁ pariharāmī’ti, ayamassa dutiyo assāso adhigato hoti.
root_text.an3.65:45.1: ‘Sace kho pana karoto karīyati pāpaṁ, na kho panāhaṁ kassaci pāpaṁ cetemi.
root_text.an3.65:45.2: Akarontaṁ kho pana maṁ pāpakammaṁ kuto dukkhaṁ phusissatī’ti, ayamassa tatiyo assāso adhigato hoti.
root_text.an3.65:46.1: ‘Sace kho pana karoto na karīyati pāpaṁ, athāhaṁ ubhayeneva visuddhaṁ attānaṁ samanupassāmī’ti, ayamassa catuttho assāso adhigato hoti.
root_text.an3.65:47.1: Sa kho so, kālāmā, ariyasāvako evaṁ averacitto evaṁ abyāpajjhacitto evaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhacitto evaṁ visuddhacitto.
root_text.an3.65:47.2: Tassa diṭṭheva dhamme ime cattāro assāsā adhigatā hontī”ti.
root_text.an3.65:48.2: Sa kho so, bhante, ariyasāvako evaṁ averacitto evaṁ abyāpajjhacitto evaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhacitto evaṁ visuddhacitto.
root_text.an3.65:48.3: Tassa diṭṭheva dhamme cattāro assāsā adhigatā honti.
root_text.an3.65:48.4: ‘Sace kho pana atthi paro loko, atthi sukatadukkaṭānaṁ kammānaṁ phalaṁ vipāko, athāhaṁ kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjissāmī’ti, ayamassa paṭhamo assāso adhigato hoti.
root_text.an3.65:49.1: ‘Sace kho pana natthi paro loko, natthi sukatadukkaṭānaṁ kammānaṁ phalaṁ vipāko, athāhaṁ diṭṭheva dhamme averaṁ abyāpajjhaṁ anīghaṁ sukhiṁ attānaṁ pariharāmī’ti, ayamassa dutiyo assāso adhigato hoti.
root_text.an3.65:50.1: ‘Sace kho pana karoto karīyati pāpaṁ, na kho panāhaṁ kassaci pāpaṁ cetemi, akarontaṁ kho pana maṁ pāpakammaṁ kuto dukkhaṁ phusissatī’ti, ayamassa tatiyo assāso adhigato hoti.
root_text.an3.65:51.1: ‘Sace kho pana karoto na karīyati pāpaṁ, athāhaṁ ubhayeneva visuddhaṁ attānaṁ samanupassāmī’ti, ayamassa catuttho assāso adhigato hoti.
root_text.an3.65:52.1: Sa kho so, bhante, ariyasāvako evaṁ averacitto evaṁ abyāpajjhacitto evaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhacitto evaṁ visuddhacitto.
root_text.an3.65:52.2: Tassa diṭṭheva dhamme ime cattāro assāsā adhigatā honti.
root_text.an3.65:53.2: ete mayaṁ, bhante, bhagavantaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāma dhammañca bhikkhusaṅghañca.
root_text.an3.65:53.3: Upāsake no, bhante, bhagavā dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupete saraṇaṁ gate”ti.

translation_text.an3.65:1.2: At one time the Buddha was wandering in the land of the Kosalans together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants when he arrived at a town of the Kālāmas named Kesamutta.
translation_text.an3.65:1.4: “It seems the ascetic Gotama—a Sakyan, gone forth from a Sakyan family—has arrived at Kesamutta.
translation_text.an3.65:1.6: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha …’
translation_text.an3.65:1.7: It’s good to see such perfected ones.”
translation_text.an3.65:2.1: Then the Kālāmas went up to the Buddha. Before sitting down to one side, some bowed, some exchanged greetings and polite conversation, some held up their joined palms toward the Buddha, some announced their name and clan, while some kept silent. Seated to one side the Kālāmas said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an3.65:3.1: “There are, sir, some ascetics and brahmins who come to Kesamutta.
translation_text.an3.65:3.2: They explain and promote only their own doctrine, while they attack, badmouth, disparage, and smear the doctrines of others.
translation_text.an3.65:3.3: Then some other ascetics and brahmins come to Kesamutta.
translation_text.an3.65:3.4: They too explain and promote only their own doctrine, while they attack, badmouth, disparage, and smear the doctrines of others.
translation_text.an3.65:3.5: So, sir, we’re doubting and uncertain:
translation_text.an3.65:3.6: ‘I wonder who of these respected ascetics and brahmins speaks the truth, and who speaks falsehood?’”
translation_text.an3.65:3.7: “It is enough, Kālāmas, for you to be doubting and uncertain.
translation_text.an3.65:3.8: Doubt has come up in you about an uncertain matter.
translation_text.an3.65:4.1: Please, Kālāmas, don’t go by oral transmission, don’t go by lineage, don’t go by testament, don’t go by canonical authority, don’t rely on logic, don’t rely on inference, don’t go by reasoned train of thought, don’t go by the acceptance of a view after deliberation, don’t go by the appearance of competence, and don’t think ‘The ascetic is our respected teacher.’
translation_text.an3.65:4.2: But when you know for yourselves:
translation_text.an3.65:4.3: ‘These things are unskillful, blameworthy, criticized by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to harm and suffering’, then you should give them up.
translation_text.an3.65:5.2: Does greed come up in a person for their welfare or harm?”
translation_text.an3.65:7.1: “A greedy individual, overcome by greed, kills living creatures, steals, commits adultery, lies, and encourages others to do the same. Is that for their lasting harm and suffering?”
translation_text.an3.65:9.2: Does hate come up in a person for their welfare or harm?”
translation_text.an3.65:11.1: “A hateful individual, overcome by hate, kills living creatures, steals, commits adultery, lies, and encourages others to do the same. Is that for their lasting harm and suffering?”
translation_text.an3.65:13.2: Does delusion come up in a person for their welfare or harm?”
translation_text.an3.65:15.1: “A deluded individual, overcome by delusion, kills living creatures, steals, commits adultery, lies, and encourages others to do the same. Is that for their lasting harm and suffering?”
translation_text.an3.65:17.1: “What do you think, Kālāmas, are these things skillful or unskillful?”
translation_text.an3.65:23.1: “When you undertake them, do they lead to harm and suffering, or not?
translation_text.an3.65:23.2: Or how do you see this?”
translation_text.an3.65:24.1: “When you undertake them, they lead to harm and suffering.
translation_text.an3.65:25.3: don’t go by oral transmission, don’t go by lineage, don’t go by testament, don’t go by canonical authority, don’t rely on logic, don’t rely on inference, don’t go by reasoned train of thought, don’t go by the acceptance of a view after deliberation, don’t go by the appearance of competence, and don’t think “The ascetic is our respected teacher.”
translation_text.an3.65:25.4: But when you know for yourselves:
translation_text.an3.65:25.5: “These things are unskillful, blameworthy, criticized by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to harm and suffering”, then you should give them up.’
translation_text.an3.65:25.6: That’s what I said, and this is why I said it.
translation_text.an3.65:26.1: Please, Kālāmas, don’t go by oral transmission, don’t go by lineage, don’t go by testament, don’t go by canonical authority, don’t rely on logic, don’t rely on inference, don’t go by reasoned train of thought, don’t go by the acceptance of a view after deliberation, don’t go by the appearance of competence, and don’t think ‘The ascetic is our respected teacher.’
translation_text.an3.65:26.2: But when you know for yourselves:
translation_text.an3.65:26.3: ‘These things are skillful, blameless, praised by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to welfare and happiness’, then you should acquire them and keep them.
translation_text.an3.65:27.2: Does contentment come up in a person for their welfare or harm?”
translation_text.an3.65:29.1: “An individual who is content, not overcome by greed, doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit adultery, lie, or encourage others to do the same. Is that for their lasting welfare and happiness?”
translation_text.an3.65:31.2: Does love come up in a person for their welfare or harm? …
translation_text.an3.65:31.3: Does understanding come up in a person for their welfare or harm? …
translation_text.an3.65:31.4: Is that for their lasting welfare and happiness?”
translation_text.an3.65:33.1: “What do you think, Kālāmas, are these things skillful or unskillful?”
translation_text.an3.65:39.1: “When you undertake them, do they lead to welfare and happiness, or not?
translation_text.an3.65:39.2: Or how do you see this?”
translation_text.an3.65:40.1: “When you undertake them, they lead to welfare and happiness.
translation_text.an3.65:41.3: don’t go by oral transmission, don’t go by lineage, don’t go by testament, don’t go by canonical authority, don’t rely on logic, don’t rely on inference, don’t go by reasoned train of thought, don’t go by the acceptance of a view after deliberation, don’t go by the appearance of competence, and don’t think “The ascetic is our respected teacher.”
translation_text.an3.65:41.4: But when you know for yourselves:
translation_text.an3.65:41.5: “These things are skillful, blameless, praised by sensible people, and when you undertake them, they lead to welfare and happiness”, then you should acquire them and keep them.’
translation_text.an3.65:41.6: That’s what I said, and this is why I said it.
translation_text.an3.65:42.1: Then that noble disciple is rid of desire, rid of ill will, unconfused, aware, and mindful. They meditate spreading a heart full of love to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of love to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.
translation_text.an3.65:42.2: They meditate spreading a heart full of compassion to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of compassion to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.
translation_text.an3.65:42.3: They meditate spreading a heart full of rejoicing to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of rejoicing to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.
translation_text.an3.65:42.4: They meditate spreading a heart full of equanimity to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of equanimity to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.
translation_text.an3.65:43.1: When that noble disciple has a mind that’s free of enmity and ill will, uncorrupted and purified,
translation_text.an3.65:43.2: they’ve won four consolations in this very life.
translation_text.an3.65:43.3: ‘If it turns out there is another world, and good and bad deeds have a result, then—when the body breaks up, after death—I’ll be reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.’ This is the first consolation they’ve won.
translation_text.an3.65:44.1: ‘If it turns out there is no other world, and good and bad deeds don’t have a result, then in this very life I’ll keep myself free of enmity and ill will, untroubled and happy.’ This is the second consolation they’ve won.
translation_text.an3.65:45.1: ‘If it turns out that bad things happen to people who do bad things, then since I have no bad intentions,
translation_text.an3.65:45.2: and since I’m not doing anything bad, how can suffering touch me?’ This is the third consolation they’ve won.
translation_text.an3.65:46.1: ‘If it turns out that bad things don’t happen to people who do bad things, then I still see myself pure on both sides.’ This is the fourth consolation they’ve won.
translation_text.an3.65:47.1: When that noble disciple has a mind that’s free of enmity and ill will, undefiled and purified,
translation_text.an3.65:47.2: they’ve won these four consolations in this very life.”
translation_text.an3.65:48.1: “That’s so true, Blessed One! That’s so true, Holy One!
translation_text.an3.65:48.2: When that noble disciple has a mind that’s free of enmity and ill will, undefiled and purified,
translation_text.an3.65:48.3: they’ve won these four consolations in this very life. …
translation_text.an3.65:53.2: We go for refuge to the Buddha, to the teaching, and to the mendicant Saṅgha.
translation_text.an3.65:53.3: From this day forth, may the Buddha remember us as lay followers who have gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.an3.65:3.2: parappavādaṁ → paravādaṁ (bj, sya-all) | paribhavanti omakkhiṁ → opapakkhiṁ (si, sya-all, km, pts1ed), omakkhikaṁ (mr)
variant_text.an3.65:3.8: Kaṅkhanīyeva pana → kaṅkhanīye ca pana (bj); kaṅkhāniyeva pana (sya-all, pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.65:7.1: tathattāya → tadatthāya (mr) | yaṁ sa → yaṁ tassa (mr)
variant_text.an3.65:42.1: Sa kho so → yo kho (mr) | patissato → sato (mr)
variant_text.an3.65:43.3: sukatadukkaṭānaṁ → sukaṭadukkaṭānaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | athāhaṁ → ṭhānamahaṁ (bj, pts1ed); ṭhānametaṁ yenāhaṁ (sya-all, km)
variant_text.an3.65:44.1: athāhaṁ → idhāhaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | sukhiṁ → sukhaṁ (bj); sukhī (sya-all, km)
